Project Phoenix - Quarterly Status Report

Overview
Project Phoenix is the company's effort to migrate its customer-facing web
platform from a monolithic Java application to a set of microservices written
in Go. The migration began eighteen months ago and is scheduled to finish in
two more quarters.

Progress this quarter
The team completed the rewrite of the authentication, billing, and search
services. All three are now running in production and serving live traffic
behind a feature flag for ten percent of users. Latency for sign-in dropped
from 480 milliseconds to 120 milliseconds, and search relevance improved by
fifteen percent on the standard evaluation set. No customer-facing incidents
were caused by the new services.

Open risks
Two risks remain. First, the legacy billing database is still the system of
record, and the new billing service writes to it through a thin adapter. If
the adapter falls behind during peak traffic, invoices can be delayed by up
to an hour. Second, the team is short one senior engineer after a recent
departure, which puts the remaining migration work on a tight schedule.

Next quarter
The plan for next quarter is to migrate the notifications and reporting
services, raise the production traffic share for the three completed services
to fifty percent, and hire a replacement senior engineer. If all three goals
are met, the project remains on track for completion in the following quarter.
